modelIdentification.name: SISO-STD-001.1-2025 - Real-time Platform Reference Communication FOM Module
modelIdentification.purpose: The RPR FOM supports interoperability for real-time, platform oriented defense simulation.
modelIdentification.description: The Communication module is used to simulate radio transmitters and receivers as well as the radio signals that are transmitted between them.

reference.identification: SISO-STD-001.1-2025 - Real-time Platform Reference Foundation FOM Module
SISO-STD-001.1-2025 - Real-time Platform Reference Enumerations FOM Module
SISO-STD-001.1-2025 - Real-time Platform Reference Base FOM Module
Standard for Guidance, Rationale, and Interoperability Modalities for the Real-time Platform Reference Federation Object Model (RPR FOM) SISO-STD-001-2025 4 November 2025
IEEE Standard for Distributed Interactive Simulation - Application Protocols IEEE Std 1278.1-2012 19 December 2012

modelIdentification.other:

Board of Directors.
Documentation: SISO hereby grants a general, royalty-free license to copy, distribute, display, and make derivative works from this material, for noncommercial purposes, provided that any use of the material contains the following attribution: “Reprinted with permission from SISO Inc.” The material may not be used for a commercial purpose without express written permission from the SISO Inc. Board of Directors.
SISO Inc. Board of Directors 7901 4th St N, Suite 300-4043 St Petersburg FL 33702 USA

objectClass.name: RadioTransmitter
objectClass.semantics: A device that sends out information encoded in electromagnetic waves in the radio frequency range.

attribute.name: AntennaPatternData
attribute.semantics: The radiation pattern of the radio's antenna.

attribute.name: CryptographicMode
attribute.semantics: The mode of the cryptographic system.

attribute.name: CryptoSystem
attribute.semantics: The type of cryptographic equipment in use.

attribute.name: EncryptionKeyIdentifier
attribute.semantics: The identification of the key used to encrypt the radio signals being transmitted. The transmitter and receiver should be considered to be using the same key if these numbers match.

attribute.name: Frequency
attribute.semantics: The center frequency of the radio transmissions.

attribute.name: FrequencyBandwidth
attribute.semantics: The bandpass of the radio transmissions.

attribute.name: FrequencyHopInUse
attribute.semantics: Whether the radio is using frequency hopping or not.

attribute.name: HighFidelityHAVEQUICKData
attribute.semantics: Additional information related to High Fidelity HAVE QUICK and SATURN radios.

attribute.name: Power
attribute.semantics: The EIRP when transmitting.

attribute.name: PseudoNoiseInUse
attribute.semantics: Whether the radio is using pseudo noise or not.

attribute.name: RadioAttachedIndicator
attribute.semantics: Indicates whether the radio transmitter is attached to an entity or object.

attribute.name: RadioIndex
attribute.semantics: A number that uniquely identifies this radio from others on the host.

attribute.name: RadioInputSource
attribute.semantics: Specifies which position or data port provided the input for the transmission.

attribute.name: RadioSystemType
attribute.semantics: The type of radio transmitter.

attribute.name: RFModulationSystemParameters
attribute.semantics: Classification of the modulation system type and parameters.

attribute.name: RFModulationType
attribute.semantics: Classification of the modulation type.

attribute.name: StreamTag
attribute.semantics: A globally unique identifier for the associated audio stream.

attribute.name: TimeHopInUse
attribute.semantics: Whether the radio is using time hopping or not.

attribute.name: TransmitterOperationalStatus
attribute.semantics: The current operational state of the radio transmitter.

attribute.name: WorldLocation
attribute.semantics: The location of the antenna in the world coordinate system.

objectClass.name: RadioReceiver
objectClass.semantics: A device that converts incoming electromagnetic waves in the radio frequency range into information.

attribute.name: RadioAttachedIndicator
attribute.semantics: Indicates whether the radio receiver is attached to an entity or object.

attribute.name: RadioIndex
attribute.semantics: A number that uniquely identifies this radio receiver from other receivers on the host object.

attribute.name: ReceivedPower
attribute.semantics: The radio frequency power received, after applying any propagation loss and antenna gain.

attribute.name: ReceivedTransmitterIdentifier
attribute.semantics: The object instance ID of the transmitter that generated the received radio signal.

attribute.name: ReceiverOperationalStatus
attribute.semantics: The operational state of the radio receiver.

interactionClass.name: RadioSignal
interactionClass.semantics: The wireless transmission and reception of audio or digital data by means of electromagnetic waves.

interactionClass.name: EncodedAudioRadioSignal
interactionClass.semantics: A radio signal used to transmit voice/audio data encoded according to a standard encoding scheme.

parameter.name: AudioData
parameter.semantics: The data associated with the encoded audio radio signal.

interactionClass.name: RawBinaryRadioSignal
interactionClass.semantics: A radio signal used to transmit raw binary data.

parameter.name: HostRadioIndex
parameter.semantics: The object instance ID of the radio transmitting this signal.

parameter.name: DataRate
parameter.semantics: The rate at which the data is being transmitted.

parameter.name: SignalDataLength
parameter.semantics: The length of the signal data.

parameter.name: SignalData
parameter.semantics: The signal data.

parameter.name: TacticalDataLinkType
parameter.semantics: The type of tactical data link used to transmit this signal (if any).

parameter.name: TDLMessageCount
parameter.semantics: The number of tactical data link messages contained in this signal.

interactionClass.name: DatabaseIndexRadioSignal
interactionClass.semantics: A radio signal that represents transmission of a pre-determined message by using a pre-defined database index.

parameter.name: HostRadioIndex
parameter.semantics: The object instance ID of the radio transmitting this signal.

parameter.name: DatabaseIndex
parameter.semantics: The index into the federation specific database of stored signals.

parameter.name: Duration
parameter.semantics: The duration of the stored signal to be replayed.

parameter.name: StartOffset
parameter.semantics: The offset, from the start of the stored signal, that the signal is replayed from.

parameter.name: TacticalDataLinkType
parameter.semantics: The type of tactical data link used to transmit this signal (if any).

parameter.name: TDLMessageCount
parameter.semantics: The number of tactical data link messages contained in this signal.

interactionClass.name: ApplicationSpecificRadioSignal
interactionClass.semantics: A radio signal that can be used for an application specific encoding scheme.

parameter.name: HostRadioIndex
parameter.semantics: The object instance ID of the radio transmitting this signal.

parameter.name: DataRate
parameter.semantics: The rate at which the data is being transmitted.

parameter.name: SignalDataLength
parameter.semantics: The length of the signal data.

parameter.name: SignalData
parameter.semantics: The signal data.

parameter.name: TacticalDataLinkType
parameter.semantics: The type of tactical data link used to transmit this signal (if any).

parameter.name: TDLMessageCount
parameter.semantics: The number of tactical data link messages contained in this signal.

parameter.name: UserProtocolID
parameter.semantics: The ID of the user protocol in use.

simpleData.name: BitRateBitPerSecondUnsignedInteger32
simpleData.semantics: Rate of transmission, in bits per second.

simpleData.name: FrequencyHertzUnsignedInteger64
simpleData.semantics: Frequency of a radio transmission, in hertz.

simpleData.name: NetNumberUnsignedInteger16
simpleData.semantics: Net number, ranging from 0 to 999 decimal.

simpleData.name: SampleRateHertzUnsignedInteger32
simpleData.semantics: Rate of sampling, in samples per second.

simpleData.name: TimeHundredsOfMicrosecondInteger32
simpleData.semantics: Time, based on SI base unit second, expressed in hundreds of microseconds.

simpleData.name: WordOfDayUnsignedInteger32
simpleData.semantics: A Word of Day (WOD) contains six decimal digits. The first digit can only be a 2 or a 3, while the second third and fourth can be 0 through 9. The last two digits can only be 00, 25, 50, or 75. WOD notation has a decimal between the third and fourth digit. Example notation: "300.025".

arrayData.name: HighFidelityHAVEQUICKStructArray
arrayData.semantics: Array with High Fidelity HAVE QUICK data.

arrayData.name: SignalDataArray
arrayData.semantics: The audio or digital data conveyed in a radio transmission.

fixedRecordData.name: AudioDataTypeStruct
fixedRecordData.semantics: Specifies an encoded audio radio signal.

field.name: StreamTag
field.semantics: Identifier for the audio stream. This must be globally unique.

field.name: EncodingType
field.semantics: The type of encoding used for the audio data.

field.name: SampleRate
field.semantics: The number of samples per second in the audio data.

field.name: DataLength
field.semantics: The length of the signal data.

field.name: SampleCount
field.semantics: The number of samples in this transmission.

field.name: Data
field.semantics: The signal data.

fixedRecordData.name: BasicHAVEQUICKModulationStruct
fixedRecordData.semantics: Modulation parameters for Basic HAVE QUICK radio system.

field.name: NETID
field.semantics: The NET ID being used.

field.name: MWODIndex
field.semantics: This is an index into the "MWOD Index - WOD Correlation Table" provided by the exercise agreement. When exercise agreement data is not available or MWOD Index is not known, the default maintenance and training MWOD Index value of 1 shall be used, which corresponds to the WOD values in Table C.2 of IEEE Std 1278.1-2012 Section C.4.2.2.

field.name: TimeOfDay
field.semantics: The Time of Day (TOD) field for the Basic HAVE QUICK MP record is used to specify a representative Time of Day value for a given radio. A value of zero shall be considered a match to any other value received.

fixedRecordData.name: CCTTSINCGARSModulationStruct
fixedRecordData.semantics: Modulation parameters for CCTT SINCGARS radio system.

field.name: FHNetID
field.semantics: Frequency hopping network identifier.

field.name: HopSetID
field.semantics: Identification for the set of frequencies used when creating a hopping pattern.

field.name: LockoutSetID
field.semantics: Identification for the set of frequencies that are excluded from the hopping pattern.

field.name: StartOfMessage
field.semantics: Specifies whether the radio is starting or continuing a transmission. A CCTT SINCGARS radio shall not receive any part of an FH transmission if the Start of Message is missed. Start of message may be missed if the radio was transmitting, receiving a different radio, or otherwise not ready to start receiving a transmission.

field.name: ClearChannel
field.semantics: Specifies that the transmission is not subject to propagation loss, interference, COMSEC, or any other form of signal degradation. Clear channels are used for interoperability with older systems and for control purposes.

field.name: FHSynchronizationTimeOffset
field.semantics: The offset to exercise time in seconds for the clock in the radio.

field.name: TransmissionSecurityKey
field.semantics: Identifies the transmission security key that is used when generating the hopping pattern.

fixedRecordData.name: HighFidelityHAVEQUICKStruct
fixedRecordData.semantics: Specification of a High Fidelity HAVE QUICK record. See IEEE Std 1278.1-2012 Section C.4.2.3 for more information.

field.name: NETID
field.semantics: Contains the NET ID being used.

field.name: TODTransmitIndicator
field.semantics: Indicates whether a TOD is being transmitted. This is also referred to as a Mickey.

field.name: TODDelta
field.semantics: Specifies the difference between the transceiver internal clock and the reference clock, which is the amount of clock drift that has occurred. This field is expressed in units of hundreds of microseconds. The reference clock is an internally simulated clock that simulates a drift or difference from the reference value of zero, with which all simulated HAVE QUICK radios are assumed to be synchronized. This drift shall be placed in the TOD Delta field of the transmitting radio. Receiving radios may then use the TOD Delta field to emulate audio effects (such as clipped or garbled audio) due to HAVE QUICK time mismatches. If the TOD Delta field is zero, the radio is considered to be synchronized with the reference clock for all of the High-Fidelity HAVE QUICK radios participating in the exercise.

field.name: WOD1
field.semantics: Word of Day (WOD) 1. All six WODs collectively are called a Multiple Word of Day (MWOD). May also be specified as segment 1 or CH20.

field.name: WOD2
field.semantics: Word of Day (WOD) 2. All six WODs collectively are called a Multiple Word of Day (MWOD). May also be specified as segment 2 or CH19.

field.name: WOD3
field.semantics: Word of Day (WOD) 3. All six WODs collectively are called a Multiple Word of Day (MWOD). May also be specified as segment 3 or CH18.

field.name: WOD4
field.semantics: Word of Day (WOD) 4. All six WODs collectively are called a Multiple Word of Day (MWOD). May also be specified as segment 4 or CH17.

field.name: WOD5
field.semantics: Word of Day (WOD) 5. All six WODs collectively are called a Multiple Word of Day (MWOD). May also be specified as segment 5 or CH16.

field.name: WOD6
field.semantics: Word of Day (WOD) 6. All six WODs collectively are called a Multiple Word of Day (MWOD). May also be specified as segment 6 or CH15.

fixedRecordData.name: NETIDStruct
fixedRecordData.semantics: The NET ID for HAVE QUICK radios.

field.name: NetNumber
field.semantics: The net number ranging from 0 to 999 decimal.

field.name: FrequencyTable
field.semantics: The Frequency Table portion of the NET ID in use.

field.name: Mode
field.semantics: The mode.

fixedRecordData.name: RadioTypeStruct
fixedRecordData.semantics: Type of radio, based on the Radio Type record as specified in IEEE Std 1278.1-2012 Section 6.2.71.

field.name: EntityKind
field.semantics: The kind of the entity, set to Radio (7).

field.name: Domain
field.semantics: The domain that the radio normally operates in.

field.name: CountryCode
field.semantics: The country to which the design of the radio entity is attributed.

field.name: Category
field.semantics: The primary type of information that is transmitted or received by the radio when in operation.

field.name: Subcategory
field.semantics: The Radio Nomenclature System associated with the radio identified in the Specific field (e.g., a JETDS PRC-series radio).

field.name: Specific
field.semantics: The specific radio associated with the Radio Nomenclature System contained in the Subcategory field.

field.name: Extra
field.semantics: The version or variation of the radio.

variantRecordData.name: AntennaPatternVariantStruct

alternative.name: IsotropicSphericalRadiationPattern
alternative.semantics: No Antenna Pattern Type and Antenna Pattern data. See IEEE Std 1278.1-2012 Section 5.8.3.3 item b5.

alternative.name: BeamAntenna
alternative.semantics: Specifies the direction, pattern, and polarization of radiation from a radio transmitter's antenna, represented with respect to a beam coordinate system.

variantRecordData.semantics: Specifies the radiation pattern from the antenna, its orientation in space, and the polarization of the radiation.

variantRecordData.name: RFModulationSystemParametersVariantStruct

alternative.name: GenericRadioOrSimpleIntercom
alternative.semantics: No Modulation Parameter data. See IEEE Std 1278.1-2012 Section C.3.2.2.

alternative.name: CCTTSINCGARSModulation
alternative.semantics: Modulation parameters for CCTT SINCGARS radio system.

alternative.name: BasicHAVEQUICKModulation
alternative.semantics: Modulation parameters for HAVE QUICK or SATURN radio system.

variantRecordData.semantics: Specifies the system type as well as modulation parameters specific to the type.

variantRecordData.name: RFModulationTypeVariantStruct

alternative.name: NoStatementModulationType
alternative.semantics: Detail for a major modulation type for which no statement is provided. This alternative is also used to implement a Simple Intercom capability using RadioTransmitter objects, see IEEE Std 1278.1 Section C.3. Then the value indicates the intercom priority in the range 0 to 255, with 0 = Exempt - Always heard regardless of the highest priority in use 1 = Highest Priority 255 = Lowest Priority

alternative.name: AmplitudeModulationType
alternative.semantics: Detailed modulation type for the amplitude major modulation type.

alternative.name: AmplitudeAngleModulationType
alternative.semantics: Detailed modulation type for the amplitude and angle major modulation type.

alternative.name: AngleModulationType
alternative.semantics: Detailed modulation type for the angle major modulation type.

alternative.name: CombinationModulationType
alternative.semantics: Detailed modulation type for the combination major modulation type.

alternative.name: PulseModulationType
alternative.semantics: Detailed modulation type for the pulse major modulation type.

alternative.name: UnmodulatedType
alternative.semantics: Detailed modulation type for the unmodulated major modulation type.

alternative.name: CarrierPhaseShiftModulationType
alternative.semantics: Detailed modulation type for the Carrier Phase Shift Modulation (CPSM) major modulation type.

alternative.name: SATCOMModulationType
alternative.semantics: Detailed modulation type for the SATCOM major modulation type. Note that this is a low-fidelity representation of SATCOM modulation.

variantRecordData.semantics: Specifies the major modulation type as well as certain detailed information specific to the type.

note.label: RPRnoteCommunication1
note.semantics: This attribute is updated if the current value differs from the previously updated value by more than the value specified by the symbolic name (default value: 500m).

note.label: RPRnoteCommunication2
note.semantics: This must reference a valid Object instance.

note.label: RPRnoteCommunication3
note.semantics: All the fields in the radio type struct are enumerations. The values for the individual fields (that do not use enumeration datatypes from within the RPR FOM) are to be derived from the federation agreements, which could refer to SISO-REF-010.
